title: The influence of agricultural tillage practices on soil biodiversity: Soil metagenomic methods, arthropod community

steps: Bulk soil samples were collected to a depth of 15cm using a trowel and placed in 2L plastic bags.

Samples were placed in BioQuip Collapsible Berlese funnels with the only modification being an addition of a two-ply layer of cheese cloth to the metal grate. Collapsible Berlese Funnel BioQuip Catalog #2832
60W light bulbs were used to dry the soil samples until they felt completely desiccated (~1 week).

After fixing, 100% ethanol was added to increase the concentration to 95% and the samples were stored at -20 ºC. Since only eight Berlese funnels were available, half of the bulk soil samples were stored at 4 ºC for ~1 week.
Arthropods were visually sorted under a dissecting microscope and identified to the taxonomic levels of class or order
Larger arthropods such as Coleoptera or Diplopoda were sub-sampled by removing either a leg or a few body segments. For smaller Arthropods such as mites and Collembola, entire organisms were used.
All organisms from a sample were placed together in a 1.5mL Eppendorf tube in 95% ethanol and stored at -20 ºC until DNA extraction.
Ethanol was removed from all tubes using a heated SpeedVac centrifugal evaporator.
The glass fiber insect DNA extraction protocol developed for the Canadian Center for DNA Barcoding was used (supplementary methods)

The MO BIO PowerMax Soil DNA isolation kit was chosen as it contains two inhibitor precipitation reagents and was used for the microbial soil samples from the study PowerMax® Soil DNA Isolation Kit Mobio Catalog #12988-10

All reagent volumes were scaled down by a factor of 0.03 so the entire volume of DNA extract could be purified using Epoch Biolab spin columns and all centrifuge spin times and speeds were adopted from the bulk Arthropod DNA extraction protocols previously outlined
All samples were eluted in 60μL of solution C6 (10mM Tris-HCL).
The high fidelity polymerase KAPA HiFi HotStart in the ReadyMix format was used. HotStart ReadyMix (KAPA HiFi PCR kit) Kapa Biosystems Catalog #KK2601
Annealing temperature gradient experiments were used to select 52 ºC, 55 ºC and 58 ºC as "low, medium and high" annealing temperatures

Reaction set-up and cycling conditions were identical to those used for microbial PCR with aside from the annealing temperatures and with 0.75μL primer cocktail (0.6μM) being used. As with microbial PCR, a negative control was set-up to monitor contamination.
Aline PCRClean DX was used to purify all amplicons, a ratio of 0.55 (beads):1 (sample) was used. PCRClean DX Aline Biosciences Catalog #C-1003-5
An Invitrogen E-gel was used to confirm purification success.
A master mix tube was prepared for each index primer. Half the total volume of master mix was added to each reaction well from either master mix tube containing the forward or reverse index primer
For PCR setup and cycling conditions see supplementary methods
Aline PCRClean DX was used for purification, but this time, at the full concentration of 1.8X (beads): 1(sample). PCRClean DX Aline Biosciences Catalog #C-1003-5
Amplicons were stored at 4 ºC to be quantified and normalized the following day.
An Invitrogen Q-Bit Fluorometer was used to measure all amplicon concentrations
All samples were normalized to 4nmol with the addition of 10mM Tris-HCL (pH 7.6).
Amplicons were then pooled and submitted to the Advanced Analysis Center Genomics Facility at The University of Guelph for Illumina MiSeq sequencing using the 250bp paired end read chemistry.
Amplicons were loaded into the MiSeq as a 6pM denatured library which was 15% PhiX.
Emerging arthropods were collected in 70% ethanol.
Amplicons were stored at 4 ºC for index PCR the following day.
creator: Jeff Strohm
